pr.title: Remove ftcommon.s and ftPe_Float.s, match ftPe_Float
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched Peach's ftPe_Float.c by replacing the assembly object with the C object and deleting the old ftPe_Float.s fallback. The PR also removed the large asm/melee/ft/ftcommon.s file. The only C-side matching adjustment in the excerpt was to introduce a small static spawnParticle helper and a local HSD_JObj* joint in ftPe_8011BB6C so the efAsync_Spawn call matched codegen. No PR body or review discussion was present.

key_files.role: Matched Peach Float C source; added static spawnParticle helper and local joint temporary around efAsync_Spawn.
Changed ftPe_Float.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Switched Peach Float object from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.s.o to src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.c.o.

what_changed: Removed asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.s, including assembly versions of ftPe_Float_CheckContinueInput, ftPe_8011BA54, ftPe_8011BAD8, ftPe_8011BB6C, ftPe_Float_Anim, ftPe_Float_IASA, ftPe_Float_Phys, and ftPe_Float_Coll.
Removed asm/melee/ft/ftcommon.s, a large assembly file containing ftCommon_* routines and local sdata/sdata2 constants.
Marked melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.c as Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Updated obj_files.mk so the build links $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.c.o instead of the old assembly object.
In ftPe_8011BB6C, split the previous inline efAsync_Spawn call into a local joint assignment and a new static spawnParticle(HSD_GObj* gobj, HSD_JObj* joint) helper.
smart_moves: Used a very small static helper around efAsync_Spawn rather than rewriting broader Peach float logic, keeping the semantic change minimal while targeting codegen.

Materialized the result of fp->parts[ftParts_8007500C(fp, FtPart_TransN)].joint into an HSD_JObj* joint local before spawning the particle; this likely helped reproduce the original call ordering/register allocation shown in the deleted assembly.
Completed the match by updating both configure.py and obj_files.mk, ensuring the C object actually replaces the asm fallback in the configured build.
Removed obsolete assembly after the matching object was enabled, reducing duplicate sources and making future work rely on the C implementation.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn call sites, especially vararg-like or effect-spawn helpers such as efAsync_Spawn, isolating the call in a static wrapper can be a useful matching lever.
Introducing an explicit local pointer for a computed argument can improve codegen compared with nesting the expression directly in the call.

When promoting a file from NonMatching to Matching, update the project metadata and object list together: configure.py controls matching status, while obj_files.mk controls whether the asm or C object is linked.
Assembly deletion PRs can be very small on the C side; the important evidence is often the object swap plus removal of the corresponding asm fallback.
naming_conventions: Peach fighter symbols use the ftPe_ prefix.
Peach float action callbacks follow the ftPe_Float_Anim, ftPe_Float_IASA, ftPe_Float_Phys, and ftPe_Float_Coll naming pattern.
Unresolved or address-based Peach helpers remained in the ftPe_8011XXXX style, e.g. ftPe_8011BB6C.
The new file-local helper used lower camel case as static spawnParticle rather than an exported ftPe_* name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Wrapped efAsync_Spawn(gobj, &fp->x60C, 0, 1236, joint) in a static helper to alter the generated call sequence.
Separated joint acquisition from the effect-spawn call: joint = fp->parts[ftParts_8007500C(fp, FtPart_TransN)].joint; spawnParticle(gobj, joint);
Converted ftPe_Float.c from NonMatching to Matching once the generated object could replace asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.s.o.
Deleted the corresponding assembly file only after the C source was selected in obj_files.mk.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided slice, so there is no human review feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that used a static helper wrapper to match efAsync_Spawn or other effect-spawn calls.
Search for remaining Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/...") entries after ftPe_Float was matched.
Check whether ftcommon.s removal was enabled by previous ftcommon.c matching work or by stale unused assembly cleanup.
Compare ftPe_Float.c call-site matching patterns with ftPe_FloatFall.c and ftPe_FloatAttack.c.
